negative_search_evidence.missing_behavior: Verifier wallet checks do not recognize FHE and therefore cannot require max-layer FHE escrow.
related_but_insufficient_code: crates/ritual-async-fees/src/wallet.rs has wallet escrow branches for LLM, HTTP/long-running HTTP, DKMS, ZK, sovereign agent, and persistent agent, but no FHE_PRECOMPILE branch.
crates/ritual-async-fees/src/lib.rs has no FHE max-layer or layer-priced escrow helper.
Dependency search found no FHE escrow or layer lockup owner in nodebuilder, alloy, alloy-evm, revm, revm-inspectors, or node.

notes: FHE metadata exists, but the wallet lockup calculation is absent.
rationale: The commit message says FHE was falling through to HTTP escrow, and the diff adds an explicit FHE_PRECOMPILE branch that calls fhe_escrow_lockup(FHE_MAX_LAYERS). No candidate in this bounded assignment contradicts or removes it.
statement: Wallet escrow validation for FHE_PRECOMPILE async calls must use fhe_escrow_lockup(FHE_MAX_LAYERS) instead of falling through to HTTP byte-based executor fee estimation.
suggested_falsification_tests: Create an AsyncCallResult with precompile_address set to FHE_PRECOMPILE and run wallet balance/lock validation with controlled balances. Assert the required lockup equals fhe_escrow_lockup(FHE_MAX_LAYERS) and differs from the HTTP fallback for the same input/output lengths.
summary: FHE wallet escrow is not ported.
test_or_falsification_candidate: Run verify_wallet_for_commitment for FHE_PRECOMPILE with otherwise sufficient state; current code returns unsupported instead of applying max-layer FHE escrow.
title: FHE wallet escrow uses max-layer lockup
